PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Apr-21-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Emilie Calhoun, Esq., for and with Lisa Kaser, Interested Party.

Robert White, Esq., and Deborah Fox, Esq., for Bushra Khan, Petitioner/Objector.

Avid Alavi, Esq., for Rebecca Owen, Special Administrator.

At the outset, the court queries whether there is any objection to the court admitting the will. No objections are presented. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated Jul-05-2022 is admitted to probate. Bushra Kahn is appointed Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Karen Ann Simons is appointed probate referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status hearing on filing either a petition for order for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. Code Sec.12200 et seq. is set for Apr-27-2026 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The court terminates Rebecca Owens' appointment as special administrator. Discussion is held re setting a status hearing on the filing of former administrator's accounting and a date is confirmed. A Status Hearing on filing of former special administrator's accounting is set for Jul-21-2025 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Apr-21-2025 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-24-2025 Continued To Apr-21-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Emilie Calhoun for Lisa Kaser, Proposed Administrator.
Ciaran O'Sullivan and Debora Fox for Bushra Khan, Petitioner/Objector.
Avid Alavi for Rebecca Owen, Special Administrator.

The Court noted recent filings from Ms. Calhoun and Mr. O'Sullivan and inquired whether there would be a will contest. Ms. Calhoun indicated that a will contest is likely, though it remains uncertain whether it will be initiated by her client or a beneficiary under the 2006 will. She raised concerns about access to the decedent's home, the location of assets, and Bushra Khan's standing as the proposed executor, alleging bias and conflicts of interest.

Mr. O'Sullivan contended that Ms. Kaser's position was inconsistent, noting that no will contest had been filed despite months of deliberation. He asserted that estate funds should not be used to conduct discovery for the opposing party and urged the Court to either admit the will to probate or require a formal will contest.

The Court acknowledged the ongoing dispute and the procedural posture of the case. The Court declined to order a judicial conference or mandatory pro bono mediation at this time, emphasizing that the key issue is whether a will contest will be filed.

The court continued the matter to April 21, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and set a deadline of April 7, 2025 for the filing of a will contest. A status hearing as to the filing of a will contest was also set for the same date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-28-2024 Continued To Jan-06-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Emily Calhoun, Esq., in person, for and with, Lisa Kaser, via Zoom videoconference, Petitioner.
Deb Fox, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, and Ciaran O'Sullivan, Esq., in person for Bushra Kahn, Objector/Beneficiary.
Avid Alavi, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for Rebecca Owen, Special Administrator.

At the court's query, Ms. Calhoun reports as to the status of a Will contest stating that she and Mr. O'Sullivan are working on resolving the issue and requests a continuance accordingly. Ms. Calhoun also points the court to the notice issue to George Benetatos. Ms. Fox advises that she is not opposed to a continuance. Mr. O'Sullivan requests the court provide a deadline for the filing of a will contest if needed. The court considers Mr. O'Sullivan's request and advises that it is inclined to continue the subject matter and is not setting a deadline to file a will contest at this time.

At the conclusion of discussion, the subject matter is continued to Jan-06-2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Belen. Not Reported.
